summary: Re-tested the three targeted fixes plus regression sanity. 7/7 backend pytest cases in test_targeted_fixes.py PASS. Frontend OutlierMap UI verified end-to-end: hover shows tooltip with title + keyword chips (font-mono pills), click navigates to /project/<pid>/doc/<docId> and DocumentReader dual-panel renders.

FIX_1_qa_fallback_citations.details: Monkey-patched qa_service.generate_json to return {answer, used_sources:[], overall_tier:'high'}. answer_question() correctly: (a) prepended 'Sumber tidak terverifikasi langsung oleh model' notice, (b) attached fallback citations with tier='low', (c) downgraded overall_tier to 'low'. The spec note 'exactly 3 citations or as many as available if fewer' is honored — BM25 may yield <3 positive-score fragments so the count is capped at min(3, gathered). Normal path (used_sources present) untouched: only cited fragments returned with model-supplied tier and overall_tier preserved.

FIX_2_matrix_cache.details: First POST /api/projects/{pid}/matrix populates db.matrix_cache with {document_id, title, cells, cached_at}. Second call returns in <1.5s (verified ~0.2s) and cells are byte-identical to first call. POST with {refresh:true} bypasses cache, calls LLM (verified slower than cache hit), and updates cached_at. DELETE /api/documents/{id} removes the matrix_cache row. DELETE /api/projects/{pid} cascades and removes matrix_cache rows for all child docs.

FIX_3_outlier_keywords.details: GET /api/projects/{pid}/outliers returns points each with a non-empty `keywords` list; stopwords ('the','yang') correctly excluded. UI: hovering [data-testid=outlier-point-<docId>] opens the .jm-tooltip with opacity:1 containing the document title, similarity %, and keyword chips styled with font-mono. Clicking the circle navigates to /project/<pid>/doc/<docId> and the DocumentReader dual-panel renders.

Regression_sanity.details: Create project, upload PDF, poll until ready, GET summary (5-8 claims with valid categories), POST evidence for claim[0] → returns >=1 evidence item — all green.

critical_code_review_comments: qa_service fallback: if BM25 returns <3 positive-score fragments, fewer than 3 citations are attached. This matches the spec's 'or as many fragments as available if fewer' clause, but UX-wise the user might see only 1-2 cites in fallback mode — acceptable.

Matrix cache invalidation is keyed only on document_id. If a document is re-uploaded with the same id (impossible today since ids are uuid), stale rows could survive. Currently safe because new uploads get fresh uuids and delete cleans up. No action needed.

OutlierMap.jsx tooltip positioning uses e.offsetX/Y relative to the SVG parent — overlapping circles (e.g. 2-doc project where both have similarity_to_centroid=1.0) overlap visually and a click can land on the topmost. Not a bug, just a known UX quirk for small projects.
seed_data_creation: Per-test PyMuPDF PDFs at /tmp/jm_targeted.pdf, /tmp/jm_delmatrix.pdf, /tmp/jm_cascademat.pdf, /tmp/ui1.pdf, /tmp/ui2.pdf. Projects all TEST_-prefixed and auto-deleted by fixture/script teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Targeted regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_targeted_fixes.py (7 cases, ~37s incl. PDF processing). Original 14-case regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_jurnalmap_api.py still applies. Each full targeted run uses ~3 LLM calls (1 doc-summary + 1 matrix initial + 1 matrix refresh). EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget healthy as of this run.
